evidence.text: Extract ALL invoice line items visible in the table.
NEVER invent values. But DO infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names. ... Use contextual reasoning.

capabilities.assessment: Effectively ALLOWED — 'extract ALL' + contextual reasoning with no artigo/SKU anchor or row-count guard. 'Do not hallucinate' is negated by infer rule. Mammafiore phantom 'Olio Noc 609' proves product invention.
capabilities.vlIncident: Mammafiore phantom Olio — 1 row across 51 aligned rows (2% hallucination rate)

evidence.text: infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names
No requirement for visible Artigo/product code column

capabilities.assessment: ALLOWED — model constructs SKU-like names from fused row cues (lot 609 + 5l*2 → 'Olio Nuto 609 10lt'). No SKU validation.
capabilities.vlIncident: Mammafiore: Olio Noc 609 / Nui Lote 609 / Olio Nuto 609 10lt — label varies per run

evidence.text: DO infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names
If quantity truly cannot be determined: quantity = null

capabilities.assessment: ALLOWED when description contains pack notation — *N, 5l*2, 1kg*2, CX*6 treated as qty source overriding column. Default qty=1 taught for EM unit.
capabilities.vlIncident: Bocconcino *6→6; Mammafiore Aceto *2→2; Rulo *2→2; Ginger Beer pack→24

evidence.text: DO infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names
Peito de Frango → unit: "kg" (inferred from product type)
Use these normalized units only when possible

capabilities.assessment: ALLOWED — unit inferred from product name/type when column unit absent or ambiguous. Normalization list encourages coercion.
capabilities.vlIncident: Mammafiore Rulo pass-c-raw: unit 'kg' inferred from 1kg in name; Ginger Beer unit semantics shift

evidence.text: Extract ALL invoice line items visible in the table
Extract all invoice line items from this restaurant invoice table image
No row-count validation, no lot/sub-line exclusion, no artigo-code anchor

capabilities.assessment: ALLOWED — completeness mandate without upper bound. Model may add rows from lot metadata, sub-lines, or fused adjacent-row cues.
capabilities.vlIncident: Mammafiore 9 rows vs 8 GT — phantom first appears in Pass C raw JSON

evidence.text: Read ... digit by digit from their respective columns. ... copy each exactly from the invoice.
Never alter quantity or unit_price to force arithmetic closure.

capabilities.assessment: NOT explicitly allowed — prompt mandates column copy. However vision misreads (not invention) produce wrong prices: Prosciutto 8.17→17.06, POMODOR 25→20. Distinction: misread ≠ invented.
capabilities.vlIncident: Column shift errors — vision limitation, not prompt-encouraged invention

evidence.text: Screenshots may have messy OCR. Use contextual reasoning.
No instruction to copy product name verbatim from description column

capabilities.assessment: ALLOWED implicitly — no 'copy name exactly' rule. OCR character noise on descriptions (8 Aviludo May rows) is non-financial but shows name reconstruction behavior.
capabilities.vlIncident: Aviludo May: Anchovas/Alconfirosa etc.; non-financial description drift

explicitlyForbiddenButOverridden: hallucination (L116) overridden by infer + contextual reasoning (L33-34)
